title: carruthersia macgregorii
intro: Carruthersia macgregorii, also known as the MacGregor's carruthersia, is a plant species in the family Moraceae. It is a climbing vine native to the tropical regions of the Philippines and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits in reducing fever, improving blood circulation, and supporting immune function. Carruthersia macgregorii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to leverage its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
